verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “To this end was I born, and for this cause came I into the world, that I should bear witness unto the truth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Every one that is of the truth heareth My Voice.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: A marvelous thing is longsuffering; it places the soul as in a quiet harbor, fleeing it from tossings and evil spirits.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this everywhere Christ hath taught us, but especially now, when He is judged, and dragged, and led about.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when He was brought to Annas, He answered with great gentleness, and, to the servant who smote Him, said what had power to bring down all his insolence; thence having gone to Caiaphas, then to Pilate, and having spent the whole night in these scenes, He all through exhibiteth His own mildness; and when they said that He was a malefactor, and were not able to prove it, He stood silent; but when He was questioned concerning the Kingdom, then He spake to Pilate, instructing him, and leading him in to higher matters.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But why was it that Pilate made the enquiry not in their presence, but apart, having gone into the judgment hall?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He suspected something great respecting Him, and wished, without being troubled by the Jews, to learn all accurately.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then when he said, “What hast thou done?” on this point Jesus made no answer; but concerning that of which Pilate most desired to hear, namely, His Kingdom, He answered, saying, “My Kingdom is not of this world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That is, “I am indeed a King, yet not such an one as thou suspectest, but far more glorious,” declaring by these words and those which follow, that no evil had been done by Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For one who saith, “To this end was I born, and for this cause came I into the world, that I should bear witness unto the truth,” showeth, that no evil hath been done by Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then when He saith, “Every one that is of the truth heareth My voice,” He draweth him on by these means, and persuadeth him to become a listener to the words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For if,” saith He, “any one is true, and desireth these things, he will certainly hear Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And, in fact, He so took him by these short words, that he said,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “What is truth?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But for the present he applieth himself to what was pressing, for he knew that this question needed time, and desired to rescue Him from the violence of the Jews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore he went out, and what said he?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I find no fault in him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Consider how prudently he acted.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He said not, “Since he hath sinned, and is deserving of death, forgive him on account of the Feast”; but having first acquitted Him of all guilt, he asks them over and above, if they were not minded to dismiss Him as innocent, yet as guilty to forgive Him on account of the time.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore he added,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Ye have a custom that I should release unto you one at the Passover”; then in a persuasory way, “Will ye therefore that I release the king of the Jews?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then cried they all, Not this man, but Barabbas.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: O accursed decision!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They demand those like mannered with themselves, and let the guilty go; but bid him punish the innocent.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this was their custom from old time.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But do thou all through observe the lovingkindness of the Lord in these circumstances.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Pilate scourged Him perhaps desiring to exhaust and to soothe the fury of the Jews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when he had not been able to deliver Him by his former measures, being anxious to stay the evil at this point, he scourged Him, and permitted to be done what was done, the robe and crown to be put on Him, so as to relax their anger.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore also he led Him forth to them crowned ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 5 ), that, seeing the insult which had been done to Him, they might recover a little from their passion, and vomit their venom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And how would the soldiers have done this, had it not been the command of their ruler?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To gratify the Jews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since it was not by his command that they at first went in by night, but to please the Jews; they dared anything for money.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But He, when so many and such things were done, yet stood silent, as He had done during the enquiry, and answered nothing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And do thou not merely hear these things, but keep them continually in thy mind, and when thou beholdest the King of the world and of all Angels, mocked of the soldiers, by words and by actions, and bearing all silently, do thou imitate Him by deeds thyself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when Pilate had called Him the King of the Jews, and they now put about Him the ap parel of mockery, then Pilate having led Him out, said,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I find no fault against him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He therefore went forth, wearing the crown.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But not even so was their rage quenched, but they cried out,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Crucify him, crucify him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then Pilate, seeing that all was done in vain, said,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Take ye him, and crucify him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Whence it is clear that he had permitted what had been done before, because of their madness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For I,” he saith, “find no fault in him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: See in how many ways the judge makes His defense, continually acquitting Him of the charges; but none of these things shamed the dogs from their purpose.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the, “Take ye him and crucify him,” is the expression of one clearing himself of the guilt, and thrusting them forward to an action not permitted to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They therefore had brought Him, in order that the thing might be done by the decision of the governor; but the contrary fell out, that He was rather acquitted than condemned by the governor’s decision.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then, because they were ashamed,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “We have,” they said, “a law, and by our law he ought to die, because he made himself the Son of God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “How then when the judge said, ‘Take ye him, and judge him according to your law,’ did ye reply, ‘It is not lawful for us to put any man to death,’ while here ye fly to the law?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And consider the charge, ‘He made himself the Son of God.’

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Tell me, is this a ground of accusation, that He who performed the deeds of the Son of God should call Himself the Son of God?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then doth Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: While they held this dialogue one with the other, He held His peace, fulfilling that saying of the Prophet, that “He openeth not his mouth: in His humiliation His judgment was taken away.” ( Isa. liii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then Pilate is alarmed when he hears from them, that He made Himself the Son of God, and dreads lest the assertion may possibly be true, and he should seem to transgress; but these men who had learnt this, both by His deeds and words, did not shudder, but are putting Him to death for the very reasons for which they ought to have worshiped Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this account he no more asks Him, “What hast thou done?” but, shaken by fear, he begins the enquiry again, saying, “Art thou the Christ?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But He answered not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he who had heard, “To this end was I born, and for this came I,” and, “My Kingdom is not of this world,” he, when he ought to have opposed His enemies and delivered Him, did not so, but seconded the fury of the Jews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then they being in every way silenced, make their cry issue in a political charge, saying, “He that maketh himself a king, speaketh against Cæsar.” ( Ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Pilate ought therefore to have accurately enquired, whether He had aimed at sovereignty, and set His hand to expel Cæsar from the kingdom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But he makes not an exact enquiry, and therefore Christ answered him nothing, because He knew that he asked all the questions idly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Besides, since His works bare witness to Him, He would not prevail by word, nor compose any defense, showing that He came voluntarily to this condition.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When He was silent, Pilate saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Knowest thou not that I have power to crucify thee?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou how he condemned himself beforehand; for, “if the whole rests with thee, why dost not thou let Him go, when thou hast found no fault in Him?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When then Pilate had uttered the sentence against himself, then He saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He that delivered Me unto thee hath the greater sin.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Showing that he also was guilty of sin.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then, to pull down his pride and arrogance, He saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Thou wouldst have no power except it were given thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Showing that this did not come to pass merely in the common order of events, but that it was accomplished mystically.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then lest, when thou hearest, “Except it were given thee,” thou shouldest deem that Pilate was exempt from all blame, on this account therefore He said, “Therefore he that delivered Me unto thee hath the greater sin.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And yet if it was given, neither he nor they were liable to any charge.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Thou objectest idly; for the ‘given’ in this place means what is ‘allowed’; as though He had said, ‘He hath permitted these things to be, yet not for that are ye clear of the wickedness.’”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He awed Pilate by the words, and proffered a clear defense.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On which account that person sought to release Him; but they again cried out, saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If thou let this man go, thou art not Cæsar’s friend.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when they profited nothing by bringing charges drawn from their own law, they wickedly betook themselves to external laws, saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Every one that maketh himself a king speaketh against Cæsar.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And where hath this Man appeared as a tyrant?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Whence can ye prove it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By the purple robe?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By the diadem?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By the dress?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By the soldiers?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Did not He ever walk unattended, save by His twelve disciples, following in every point a humble mode of living, both as to food, and clothing, and habitation?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But O what shamelessness and ill-time cowardice!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For Pilate, deeming that he should now incur some danger were he to overlook these words, comes forth as though to enquire into the matter, (for the “sitting down” showed this,) but without making any enquiry, he gave Him up to them, thinking to shame them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For to prove that he did it for this purpose, hear what he saith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Behold your king!”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But when they said, “Crucify him,” he added again, “Shall I crucify your king?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But they cried out, “We have no king but Cæsar.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Of their own will they subjected themselves to punishment; therefore also God gave them up, because they were the first to cast themselves out from His providence and superintendence; and since with one voice they rejected His sovereignty, He allowed them to fall by their own suffrages.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Still what had been said should have been sufficient to calm their passion, but they feared, lest, being let go, He should again draw the multitudes, and they did all they could to prevent this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For a dreadful thing is love of rule, dreadful and able to destroy the soul; it was on account of this that they had never heard Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And yet Pilate, in consequence of a few words, desired to let Him go, but they pressed on, saying, “Crucify him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And why did they strive to kill Him in this manner?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It was a shameful death.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Fearing therefore lest there should afterwards be any remembrance of Him, they desired to bring Him to the accursed punishment, not knowing that truth is exalted by hindrances.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To prove that they had this suspicion, listen to what they say; “We have heard that that deceiver said, After three days I will rise again” ( Matt. xxvii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 63 ); on this account they made all this stir, turning things upside down, that they might ruin matters in after time.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And the ill-ordered people, corrupted by their rulers, cried out continually, “Crucify him!”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But let us not merely read of these things, but bear them in our mind; the crown of thorns, the robe, the reed, the blows, the smiting on the cheek, the spittings, the irony.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: These things, if continually meditated on, are sufficient to take down all anger; and if we be mocked at, if we suffer injustice, let us still say, “The servant is not greater than his Lord” ( c. xiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 16 ); and let us bring forward the words of the Jews, which they uttered in their madness, saying, “Thou art a Samaritan, and hast a devil” ( c. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 48 ); and, “He casteth out devils by Beelzebub.” ( Luke xi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For on this account He bare all these things, in order that we might walk in His footsteps, and endure those mockings which disturb more than any other kind of reproach.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet nevertheless He not only bare these things, but even used every means to save and deliver from the appointed punishment those who did them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He sent the Apostles also for their salvation, at least thou hearest them saying, that, “We know that through ignorance ye did it” ( Acts iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 17 ); and by these means drawing them to repentance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This let us also imitate; for nothing so much maketh God propitious as the loving enemies, and doing good to those who despitefully use us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When a man insults thee, look not to him, but to the devil who moves him, and against him empty all thy wrath, but pity the man who is moved by him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if lying is from the devil, to be angry without a cause is much more so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When thou seest one turning another into ridicule, consider that it is the devil who moves him, for mockings belong not to Christians.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he who hath been bidden to mourn, and hath heard, “Woe, ye that laugh” ( Luke vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 25 ), and who after this insults, and jests, and is excited, demands not reproach from us, but sorrow, since Christ also was troubled when He thought on Judas.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: All these things therefore let us practice in our actions, for if we act not rightly in these, we have come to no purpose and in vain into the world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Or rather we have come to our harm, for faith is not sufficient to bring men to the Kingdom, nay, it even hath power in this way most to condemn those who exhibit an ill life; for He “which knew his Lord’s will, and did it not, shall be beaten with many stripes” ( Luke xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 47 ); and again, “If I had not come and spoken unto them, they had not had sin.” ( c. xv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What excuse then shall we have, who have been set within the palace, and deemed worthy to stoop down and enter into the sanctuary, and have been made partakers of the releasing Mysteries, and who yet are worse than the Greeks, who have shared in none of these things?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if they for the sake of vainglory have shown so much true wisdom, much more ought we to go after all virtue, because it is pleasing to God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But at present we do not even despise wealth; while they have often been careless of their life, and in wars have given up their children to their madness about devils, and have despised nature for the sake of their devils, but we do not even despise money for the sake of Christ, nor anger on account of God’s will, but are inflamed, and in no better state than the fevered.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And just as they, when possessed by their malady, are all burning, so we, suffocated as by some fire, can stop at no point of desire, increasing both anger and avarice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this account I am ashamed and astonished, when I behold among the Greeks men despising riches, but all mad among ourselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For even if we could find some despising riches, we should find that they have been made captive by other vices, by passion or envy; and a hard thing it is to discover true wisdom without a blemish.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But the reason is, that we are not earnest to get our remedies from the Scriptures, nor do we apply ourselves to those Scriptures with compunction, and sorrow, and groaning, but carelessly, if at any time we chance to be at leisure.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore when a great rush of worldly matters comes, it overwhelms all; and if there hath been any profit, destroys it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if a man have a wound, and after putting on a plaster, do not tie it tight, but allow it to fall off, and expose his sore to wet, and dust, and heat, and ten thousand other things able to irritate it, he will get no good; yet not by reason of the inefficacy of the remedies, but by reason of his own carelessness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this also is wont to happen to us, when we attend but little to the divine oracles, but give ourselves up wholly and incessantly to things of this life; for thus all the seed is choked, and all is made unfruitful.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That this may not be the case, let us look carefully a little, let us look up to heaven, let us bend down to the tombs and coffins of the departed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the same end awaiteth us, and the same necessity of departure will often come upon us before the evening.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Prepare we then for this expedition; there is need of many supplies for the journey, for great is the heat there, and great the drought, and great the solitude.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Henceforth there is no reposing at an inn, there is no buying anything, when one hath not taken all from hence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hear at least what the virgins say, “Go ye to them that sell” ( Matt. xxv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 9 ); but they who went found not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hear what Abraham saith, “A gulf between us and you.” ( Luke xvi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hear what Ezekiel saith concerning that day, that Noah, and Job, and Daniel shall in nowise deliver their sons. ( Ezek. xiv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But may it never come to pass that we hear these words, but that having taken hence sufficient provision for our way to eternal life, we may behold with boldness our Lord Jesus Christ, with whom to the Father and the Holy Ghost be glory, dominion, honor, now and ever, and world without end.
